title: AgentBox box configuration
description: Declarative config consumed by agentbox-ctl (the in-container supervisor). Sits at /workspace/agentbox.yaml.

services.description: Map of service name → service spec. Names must match [A-Za-z0-9_-]+.

tasks.description: Map of task name → task spec. Tasks are one-shot units that run before dependent services (via `needs:`).

ide.description: VS Code attach customizations. Read host-side by `agentbox code`; the supervisor ignores it. Shape is intentionally permissive in this PR.

defaults.description: Host-side AgentBox layered-config defaults for this project. Same shape as ~/.agentbox/config.yaml. The supervisor ignores this; @agentbox/config validates it strictly when loaded. See https://agentbox.dev/schema/user-config.schema.json for the full key set.

command.description: Shell string (run via `bash -c`) or argv array. Non-empty.

env.description: Extra environment variables. Values must be scalars (string, number, or boolean) — they are coerced to strings.

needs.description: Names of tasks or services that must reach their terminal-good state before this unit starts.

cwd.description: Working directory. Relative paths resolve against /workspace.

autostart.description: Start automatically when the daemon boots.

restart.description: When to restart after the process exits. `always` ignores exit code, `on-failure` (default) restarts only on non-zero, `never` leaves it dead.

expose.description: Mark this as THE web service. AgentBox forwards container port `as` to `127.0.0.1:port` inside the box and publishes it on the host. At most one service may set this.

port.description: The port this service listens on inside the box.

as.description: Container port forwarded to `port`. Must be 80 (the only port AgentBox reserves).

ide.description: Per-service VS Code hints (read host-side by `agentbox code`). Shape is permissive in this PR.

task.description: A one-shot unit that runs to completion. Tasks cannot have restart, autostart, backoff, or ready_when.

backoff.description: Exponential backoff between restart attempts. The runtime validator additionally enforces max_ms >= initial_ms.

initial_ms.description: Delay before the first retry, in milliseconds.

max_ms.description: Cap for the retry delay, in milliseconds. Must be >= initial_ms.

factor.description: Multiplier applied to the delay after each failed restart.

ready_when.description: Readiness probe. Exactly one of port, log_match, http must be present.

port.description: TCP port the supervisor will poll on `host` (default 127.0.0.1).

host.description: Host for port probes. Defaults to 127.0.0.1. Ignored for log_match/http probes.

log_match.description: Regex matched against stdout/stderr lines. First match flips the service to `ready`.

http.description: HTTP(S) URL. Probe sends GET and waits for `expect_status` (default: any 2xx).

expect_status.description: HTTP status code the probe waits for. Only valid with `http:`.

interval_ms.description: Poll interval. Ignored for log_match probes.

initial_delay_ms.description: Wait this long after the service spawns before the first probe attempt.

timeout_ms.description: Give up after this long. Behavior on timeout is governed by `on_timeout`.

on_timeout.description: `kill` re-enters the service's restart policy. `mark_unhealthy` leaves it running but flags it (escape hatch for slow cold starts).
